2603.16497.title: Bridging the High-Frequency Data Gap: A Millisecond-Resolution Network Dataset for Advancing Time Series Foundation Models
2603.16497.paper_summary: Time series foundation models (TSFMs) require diverse, real-world datasets to adapt across varying domains and temporal frequencies. However, current large-scale datasets predominantly focus on low-frequency time series with sampling intervals, i.e., time resolution, in the range of seconds to years, hindering their ability to capture the nuances of high-frequency time series data. To address this limitation, we introduce a novel dataset that captures millisecond-resolution wireless and traffic conditions from an operational 5G wireless deployment, expanding the scope of TSFMs to incorporate high-frequency data for pre-training. Further, the dataset introduces a new domain, wireless networks, thus complementing existing more general domains like energy and finance. The dataset also provides use cases for short-term forecasting, with prediction horizons spanning from 100 milliseconds (1 step) to 9.6 seconds (96 steps). By benchmarking traditional machine learning models and TSFMs on predictive tasks using this dataset, we demonstrate that most TSFM model configurations perform poorly on this new data distribution in both zero-shot and fine-tuned settings. Our work underscores the importance of incorporating high-frequency datasets during pre-training and forecasting to enhance architectures, fine-tuning strategies, generalization, and robustness of TSFMs in real-world applications.
2603.16497.paper_summary_zh: 時間序列基礎模型（TSFMs）需要多樣化的真實世界數據集，以適應不同領域和時間頻率。然而，目前的大規模數據集主要集中在低頻時間序列上，其取樣間隔，即時間解析度，範圍從幾秒到幾年，這限制了它們捕捉高頻時間序列數據細微差別的能力。為了解決這一限制，我們引入了一個新數據集，捕捉來自運營中5G無線部署的毫秒解析度無線和交通條件，擴展了TSFMs的範疇，以納入高頻數據進行預訓練。此外，該數據集引入了一個新領域，即無線網絡，從而補充了現有的更一般領域，如能源和金融。該數據集還提供了短期預測的使用案例，預測範圍從100毫秒（1步）到9.6秒（96步）。通過在使用這個數據集的預測任務上對傳統機器學習模型和TSFMs進行基準測試，我們證明大多數TSFM模型配置在這個新數據分佈下的零樣本和微調設置中表現不佳。我們的工作強調了在預訓練和預測過程中納入高頻數據集的重要性，以增強TSFMs在實際應用中的架構、微調策略、泛化能力和穩健性。
2603.16497.author: Subina Khanal et.al.
2603.16497.authors: Subina Khanal, Seshu Tirupathi, Merim Dzaferagic, Marco Ruffini, Torben Bach Pedersen
